notes: Seasonal events roll at the END of every season and take effect the following season. Kinds: modifier (passive effect each active season), mandate (requirement checked each active season  -  penalty if unmet), contract (requirement checked each season  -  reward if met in window, optional penalty if window closes unmet), instant (one-time effect using existing event registry). Offerable contracts (is_offerable: true) appear on the player offers board and must be explicitly accepted.

events.name: Global Pandemic
events.desc: Everyone's home watching TV. Ratings tick up  -  but production costs skyrocket.

events.name: Ad Market Surge
events.desc: Brands are spending heavily. Modest income bump across all live shows.

events.name: Budget Audit
events.desc: The board dispatched accountants. Every department is haemorrhaging money on upkeep.

events.name: Tech Sector Ad Blitz
events.desc: Every startup is buying airtime. Modest income rise; Sci-Fi and News benefit a little.

events.name: Streaming Wars
events.desc: Rival platforms are poaching viewers. Everyone's cord-cutting. Views shrink across the board.

events.name: Summer Ratings Slump
events.desc: Audiences are outdoors. Every network is struggling. Views are down system-wide.

events.name: Production Strike
events.desc: Set workers walked out. Upkeep just got brutal. Views are unaffected  -  barely.

events.name: Television Golden Age
events.desc: Critics and audiences alike have rediscovered appointment TV. Views are slightly up.

events.name: National Food Obsession
events.desc: Everyone's a home chef now. Cooking shows are the talk of the nation.

events.name: Writers' Strike
events.desc: Script supply has collapsed. The union demands respect. Air at least 2 Reality shows or pay the settlement.

events.name: Public Interest Directive
events.desc: Regulators are watching. Air at least one News programme each season or face a compliance fine.

events.name: Prime Time Commitment
events.desc: Advertisers are paying a premium for Prime Time. The board insists you keep it filled.

events.name: Talent Showcase Deal
events.desc: Your agent has lined up a deal: keep at least 3 stars attached across your shows or forfeit the contract fee.

events.name: Streaming Acquisition Offer
events.desc: StreamVault wants your back catalogue. Reach 3,000 total views in the window and they'll cut a cheque.

events.name: Network Merger Talks
events.desc: A rival network is sniffing around. Show them a diverse genre lineup  -  avoid running any genre monopoly.

events.name: Live Sports Exclusivity
events.desc: A sports rights consortium wants prime-time coverage. Air 2+ Sports shows within the window.

events.name: Prestige Drama Commission
events.desc: A studio wants Drama prestige content. Keep 2+ Drama shows on air to collect the co-production fee.

events.name: Culinary Brand Sponsorship
events.desc: FreshMart wants Cooking content front and centre. Air 2+ Cooking shows within the window.

events.name: Ratings Agency Challenge
events.desc: A national ratings body will grant a prestige award and a bonus if you reach 6,000 total views.

events.name: Talent Agency Showcase
events.desc: An agency wants a showcase. Keep 4+ stars attached across your lineup for the window.

events.name: Public Broadcasting Grant
events.desc: A public broadcaster offers co-funding if you keep News on air in every season of the window.

events.name: Clip Goes Viral
events.desc: Something from your network exploded online. Modest ratings bump.

events.name: Emergency Arts Council Grant
events.desc: The government funded an emergency arts initiative. Your network qualifies for a small subsidy.

events.name: Industry Convention Buzz
events.desc: Your network was the talk of the trade show. Views and budget get a small lift.

events.name: Creative Reinvention
events.desc: A cultural moment breathes new life into your shows. Your lineup feels fresh again.
